2020: Severe depression following the sudden death of her brother. She was hospitalised for six weeks and prescribed Fluoxetine (Prozac) and Olanzapine (Zyprexa). During this time, she also began seeing a therapist for grief counselling.
2016: Experienced a manic episode characterised by excessive energy, decreased need for sleep, and impulsive decision-making. Hospitalised for three weeks and diagnosed with Bipolar Disorder. Treated with Lithium and Valproate.
2015: Another major depressive episode triggered by the anniversary of her brother's death. She was hospitalised for four weeks, during which her medications were adjusted, and she received intensive psychotherapy. Her treatment included a brief course of inpatient Electroconvulsive Therapy (ECT).

Current Hospitalisation:
Reason for Admission: Yamini was admitted following a suicide attempt involving an overdose of her prescribed medications. She has been experiencing worsening symptoms of depression and anxiety, including severe insomnia, loss of appetite, weight loss, pervasive feelings of hopelessness, and frequent panic attacks.
Length of Stay: Currently in her third week of hospitalisation in a psychiatric unit.

Medication on 2020:
Aripiprazole (Abilify) - 5 mg daily oral once after food
Zolpidem (Ambien) - 5 mg as needed once everyday, with milk orally

Medication on 2017:
Escitalopram (Lexapro) - 20 mg daily Oral, after food Once daily for 10 days
Lamotrigine (Lamictal) - 200 mg daily Oral, after food once a week for 3 weeks

Medication on 2015:
Aripiprazole (Abilify) - 10 mg daily oral for 30 days
Clonazepam (Klonopin) - 0.5 mg oral once in a week after food
Zolpidem (Ambien) - 10 mg once everyday, with milk orally for 3 months

Past Therapies:
2017 - Cognitive Behavioural Therapy (CBT) focusing on managing depressive and anxious thoughts
2016-2017 - Dialectical Be...
